content.full_text:
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P O Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

November 26, 1991

Gentlepersons:

I am seeking your professional opinion regarding my situation vis-a-vis a
potential for a conflict of interest as per the State's code.
I am an elected Director of the Mendocino City Community Services District
(MCCSD). The Board makes policies and administers all matters pertaining to
sewage and water. I am a resident of the district and therefore I am eligible
to vote on all local initiatives. I am also a businessman in town. I own and
operate together with my wife the Agate Cove Inn Bed & Breakfast.
Mendocino suffers a chronic shortage of water that has reached serious
dimensions in the current long drought. Many residents and businesses must
supplement their water needs by purchasing truck loads from a neighboring
town. A single delivery costs approximately $150 depending on the vendor.
There are several concerns regarding our water situation. I have attached a
copy of a letter that I recently sent to the Department of Fish & Game that
will provide you with a background on the entire situation.

All in the community will benefit from a municipal water system - better
fire protection (and subsequently lower insurance rates), sufficient qantity,
safer drinking water. It will also be much easier to obtain financing when
properties change hands because lenders recently demand proof of sufficient
amount of safe water. Businesses like myself will have additional benefits
such as lower liability exposure. The magnitude of benefits will vary from
business to residence and from one business to another depending on size

A suggestion was made by an opponent of the water system that I might
have a conflict of interest in voting on motions that advance the cause of
the water system. My understanding is that this would be the case only if I
was the only one benefiting from it or if my benefit was significantly
higher than anybody else in the community

Sincerely,

Jake Zahavi

AGATE COVE INN
P. O. Box 1150
Mendocino, CA 95460

Mr. Carl Wilcox
Environmental Services
Department of Fish and Game
P.O.Box 47
Yountville, CA 94599

November 6, 1991

Dear Mr Wilcox

I am the Vice President of the Mendocino Community Services District
(MCCSD) and was also elected to be the Coordinator for the New water
system. We recently asked that you give us an informal evaluation of
environment concerns that may apply to our exploratory project along the
Big River watershed. We were impressed with the professional presentation
by Ann Howald and Rick Macedo from your department.
Fish and Game and other agencies will review in the coming months various
phases of our search for, and the development of, a town water system. I
feel that providing all of you with a background on our situation will assist
in placing your efforts within the proper context.
The town of Mendocino is in dire need of a reliable source of safe water.
Currently the town relies on individual wells, of which many run dry as
early as July/August. The vast majority of the wells are shallow and are at
risk of surface contamination. Indeed a few cases of Giardia were reported.
The State Health Department in a random testing of wells found fecal
coliform contamination. The EPA cited two small water systems for not
meeting code.
As a result the State's Department of Water Resources selected Mendocino
(out of several communities) to receive a loan and grant under the 1986
Safe Water Act Bond in the amount of $4.1 million. The Board of the MCCSD
must complete several steps by May 28, 1992 in order to qualify for the
funds.
In addition to health concerns,many in the community are very concerned
about the lack of adequate fire protection. This fear has increased
dramatically in the aftermath of the recent Oakland Hills fire.
In November 1990 the residents of Mendocino went to the polls and voted on
whether we should construct a town water system. The results were 52%
Yes ; 48% No.
Residents voting against the system had primarly two concerns:
1.Cost
2.Potential for over development
Responding to these concerns the MCCSD Board of Directors passed a
resolution that puts a cap of $25 a month per household even if the cost
exceeds the estimated $4.1 million. The business community will pay the
difference.
The MCCSD has no control over development in town, but a CAC (Citizens
Advisory Committee) revised the town plan to put limits on development.

The plan has been submitted to the County Board of Supervisors for adoption.
Several expert hydrologists and geologists independently concluded that the
Big River watershed is the only viable source for a water system for the
town. Accordingly we have already drilled one test well in the alluvium
approximately a quarter of a mile up river. Unfortunately we were
disappointed both in quantity and quality.
Bearing in mind the time frame imposed on us by the State, we hired the
services of David Lawrence, an experienced hydrologist, to inventory the Big
River watershed and identify the specific sources for a town water system.
We are in the process of following Mr. Lawrence's recommendations. We
subsequently hired Mr. Bud Kamb, a land use consultant, to help us obtain the
necessary permits required by County, State and other agencies.
The Board is sensitive to potential impacts on the environment, as are the
residents in town whether they are for or against the water system. The
Board has decided in its last meeting to approve a second test drill two and
a half miles up river and the monitoring of flow in the gulches. Conversely
the Board decided to put on hold any work, even exploratory on the Big River
Laguna.
This is a very active community and I am sure that you will hear directly
from various individuals. Some are sincerely concerned about the
environment and some may merely use it as a tool to oppose change.
We as the Board have a mandate from the community to construct a
community wide water system. We strive to do so within the time
constraints imposed on us, and with the lowest possible cost, both
financially and environmentally.

Very Truly Yours,

Jake Zahavi
Vice President
Water System Coordinator


cc. See attached list.

embedding.qa_text: Fair Political Practices Commission 428 J Street, Suite 800 P.O. Box 807 Sacramento, CA 95804 November 26, 1991 Gentlepersons: I am seeking your professional opinion regarding my situation vis-a-vis a potential for a conflict of interest as per the State's code. I am an elected Director of the Mendocino City Community Services District (MCCSD). The Board makes policies and administers all matters pertaining to sewage and water. I am a resident of the district and therefore I am eligible to vote on all local initiatives. I am also a businessman in town, I own and operate together with my wife the Agate Cove Inn Bed & Breakfast. Mendocino suffers a chronic shortage of water that has reached serious dimensions in the current long drought. Many residents and businesses must supplement their water needs by purchasing truck loads from a neighboring town. A single delivery costs approximately $150 depending on the vendor. There are several concerns regarding our water situation. I have attached a copy of a letter that I recently sent to the Department of Fish & Game that will provide you with a background on the entire situation. All in the community will benefit from a municipal water system - better fire protection (and subsequently lower insurance rates), sufficient quantity, safer drinking water. It will also be much easier to obtain financing when properties change hands because lenders recently demand proof of sufficient amount of safe water. Businesses like myself will have additional benefits such as lower liability exposure. The magnitude of benefits will vary from business to residence and from one business to another depending on size. A suggestion was made by an opponent of the water system that I might have a conflict of interest in voting on motions that advance the cause of the water system. My understanding is that this would be the case only if I was the only one benefiting from it or if my benefit was significantly higher than anybody else in the community. Sincerely, Jake Zahavi Agate Cove Inn P.O. Box 1150 Mendocino, CA 95460 Mr. Carl Wilcox Environmental Services Department of Fish and Game P.O.
